preamble: The alveolar nasal [n] shifts to the voiced alveolar sibilant [z]. This typically occurs in contexts where the nasal is weakened before another consonant or in specific morphological environments, leading to the development of sibilant frication.

examples.note: Hypothesized weakening of nasals to sibilants in specific pre-consonantal contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
